# UC-22.2.29 · NIS2 Cryptography Policy Compliance — TLS and Certificate Posture

## Description

Appropriate use of cryptography protects network and information system security. This use case monitors TLS versions, weak cipher suites, and certificate expiry for public and internal services supporting NIS2-scoped workloads — aligning with organisational cryptography standards.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIS2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes stream and sourcetypes stream:tls. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Enable passive TLS inspection on critical VLAN mirrors; (2) map IPs to NIS2 services; (3) alert on weak protocols and imminent expiry; (4) integrate with PKI renewal workflow; (5) document exceptions with compensating controls. Indexes required: `index=stream`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=stream:tls`; Lookups: `nis2_scoped_service_map`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Stream (Splunkbase 1809), Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: TLS metadata from Stream, certificate inventory, `nis2_crypto_policy.csv` (min_tls_version, allowed_ciphers).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Enable passive TLS inspection on critical VLAN mirrors; (2) map IPs to NIS2 services; (3) alert on weak protocols and imminent expiry; (4) integrate with PKI renewal workflow; (5) document exceptions with compensating controls.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=stream sourcetype="stream:tls" earliest=-24h
| stats latest(tls_version) as tls_version latest(cipher) as cipher values(expiry_epoch) as cert_expiry by dest_ip dest_port
| lookup nis2_scoped_service_map dest_ip OUTPUT service_name nis2_in_scope
| where nis2_in_scope="true"
| eval weak_tls=if(match(tls_version,"SSLv|TLSv1\.0|TLSv1\.1"),1,0)
| eval cert_problem=if(cert_expiry < relative_time(now(),"+30d@d"),1,0)
| where weak_tls=1 OR cert_problem=1
| table service_name, dest_ip, dest_port, tls_version, cipher, cert_problem
```

Understanding this SPL

**NIS2 Cryptography Policy Compliance — TLS and Certificate Posture (Art. 21(2)(h))** — Appropriate use of cryptography protects network and information system security. This use case monitors TLS versions, weak cipher suites, and certificate expiry for public and internal services supporting NIS2-scoped workloads — aligning with organisational cryptography standards.

Documented **Data sources**: TLS metadata from Stream, certificate inventory, `nis2_crypto_policy.csv` (min_tls_version, allowed_ciphers). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Stream (Splunkbase 1809), Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: stream; **sourcetype**: stream:tls. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=stream, sourcetype="stream:tls", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` aggregates events by **dest_ip dest_port**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where nis2_in_scope="true"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **weak_tls** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **cert_problem** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where weak_tls=1 OR cert_problem=1` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

This use case does not use a CIM tstats shortcut; the primary SPL above is the source of truth after field normalization in your environment.

Output fields: cert_expiry, cert_problem, cipher, dest_ip, dest_port, service_name, tls_version, weak_tls.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the source system (DLP, IDP, or application) and, if this use case underpins a control test, the ServiceNow GRC console or the evidence pack for a known time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (weak TLS services), Timeline (certificate expiry), Single value (critical expiries), Bar chart (by cipher family).

## SPL

```spl
index=stream sourcetype="stream:tls" earliest=-24h
| stats latest(tls_version) as tls_version latest(cipher) as cipher values(expiry_epoch) as cert_expiry by dest_ip dest_port
| lookup nis2_scoped_service_map dest_ip OUTPUT service_name nis2_in_scope
| where nis2_in_scope="true"
| eval weak_tls=if(match(tls_version,"SSLv|TLSv1\.0|TLSv1\.1"),1,0)
| eval cert_problem=if(cert_expiry < relative_time(now(),"+30d@d"),1,0)
| where weak_tls=1 OR cert_problem=1
| table service_name, dest_ip, dest_port, tls_version, cipher, cert_problem
```

## Visualization

Table (weak TLS services), Timeline (certificate expiry), Single value (critical expiries), Bar chart (by cipher family).
